target.name: Archetype AI
target.founder: Ivan Poupyrev, Brandon Barbello, Leonardo Giusti, Jaime Lien, Nicholas Gillian

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $13M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 18 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Archetype AI is a seed-stage AI & software company founded by Ivan Poupyrev, Brandon Barbello, Leonardo Giusti, Jaime Lien, and Nicholas Gillian, having raised $13M to date. The broader AI & Software sector comprises 18 peer companies across public and private markets, including public comparable Palantir (PLTR) and private peers such as Waymo, Figure AI, Scale AI, and Applied Intuition. However, the lack of sufficiently similar seed-stage peers in narrow valuation tiers limits direct peer-median triangulation.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier to the $13M total raised proxy, reflecting typical seed-to-next-stage advancement. This yields a $32M implied valuation. A sensitivity range accounting for variance around this single-method estimate produces a low of $28M and a high of $37M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $28M to $37M, with $32M as the central estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct seed-stage comparables with sufficient depth, which prevents anchoring via peer multiples on revenue, users, or burn metrics. Further valuation refinement would require greater visibility into Archetype's current revenue run-rate, customer pipeline, or progress toward product-market fit milestones relative to its seed cohort.
